def: s.m. Chamber of a puerperal woman; lying-in chamber.
v.t. (Braj) = suwānā, or sulānā, q.v.
s.m.f. A large and bulky serpent; an enormous fabulous serpent; the basilisk.
s.f. Beauty, splendour, lustre, show; ornament, decoration, dress
subs: or sobhā-vān, adj. Beautiful; resplendent; splendid, magnificent ( = śobhāyamān, q.v.).

def: s.f. Light, lustre, radiance, splendour, brilliance; beauty, elegance, grace, comeliness; ornament, decoration, dress; — a species of the kr̤iti metre
subs: (˚bhā+an˚), s.m. The tree Hyperanthera moringa (com. called sajinā, and sahajnā (it is used as a substitute for horse-radish)
adj. (f. -ā), Beautiful, lovely; ornamented, decorated, adorned.

def: adj. See s.v. sobhā.
adj. Shining, beautiful, splendid, &c. ( = śobhī, q.v.).
adj. = شوبهن śobhan, q.v.

adj. (f. -ā), Adorning, beautifying; — shining, splendid; beautiful, handsome, pleasing; richly adorned; — propitious, auspicious; decorous, virtuous, good; — s.m. A shining, comeliness, handsomeness, pleasantness; — a burnt-offering for auspicious results; a planet; a lotus; — the fifth of the astronomical divisions of the ecliptic.
s.m. See s.v. śobhā.
adj. Shining, brilliant, splendid, resplendent; conspicuous; beautiful; adorning.
adj. = śobhamān, q.v.
adj. (f. -inī), Shining, radiant, brilliant, splendid; adorning, beautifying; beautiful, handsome; distinguished, conspicuous.
part. = شوبهت śobhit, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Beautified, adorned, decorated, ornamented, made splendid; beautiful, lovely.

ety: sobh˚ = S. शोभ(ते) rt. शुभ्; — cf. sohnā
def: v.n. To shine; to be beautified or adorned; to appear to advantage; to be becoming (to, -ko), to become, befit.
v.n. To be becoming (to, -ko), to become, befit, beseem; to grace.
s.m. Sorrow, &c. ( = śoćan, q.v.)
s.m. corr. of soć, q.v.

s.m. Thought, reflection, consideration, meditation; reverie, musing; imagination; idea; attention, regard, notice; seriousness; anxiety, concern, apprehension, perplexity; sorrow, grief; regret, penitence
subs: s.m. Apprehension and shrinking, anxiety and fear, &c. (see saṅkoć)
vulg. soć-bićār, s.m. Reflection and discrimination; consideration, discretion, caution
v.n. To be absorbed in thought or reflection; to muse

def: adj. (f. -ā), Sorrowing, lamenting; sorrowful, regretful, sad; — s.m. (f. -ā), Grieving, mourning, sorrow, grief, sadness, regret, distress.
part. adj. To be considered or pondered on; deserving or capable of consideration.
adv. On or after due consideration, deliberately, premeditatedly; wilfully.
adj. Thoughtful, considerate, meditative, serious, pensive; regardful, observant, taking note (of).
s.m. Light, lustre, radiance; flame
subs: (rays of) light for hair; having flaming locks; — epithet of the sun; of Agni; — fire.

def: part. (f. -ā), Made sad, saddened, grieved; afflicted; sorrowful.
adv. Thoughtfully, with due consideration; on or after consideration.
adv. Thoughtfully, with due consideration; on or after consideration.

ety: soć˚ = Prk. सुच्च(इ) = S. शुच्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. शुच्
def: v.t. & v.n. To think, reflect, consider, meditate, ponder on, imagine, conceive; to regard, notice, mark, observe, mind; to advert; to contrive; — to regret, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀘𑁆𑀘𑀤𑀺 (soccadi, “to purify”), from Sanskrit शोच्यते (śocyate, “to be purified; to be made to feel pain”), from the root शुच् (śuc). Cognate with Punjabi سوچنا / ਸੋਚਣਾ (socṇā), Sindhi سوچڻ (socṇ) / सोचणु (socaṇu), Nepali सोच्नु (socnu), Assamese সুচা (xusa), Odia ଶୋଚିବା (śocibā, “to grieve”).

def: s.f. See s.v. soć.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā). Born from the same womb, co-uterine; — a co-uterine brother, own brother ( = sahodar, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā). Born from the same womb, co-uterine; — a co-uterine brother, own brother ( = sahodar, q.v.).

ety: S. शोधः, rt. शुध्; or v.n. of sodhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Purification, cleaning, refining; purity, cleanliness; refinement; scrupulousness, nicety; sanctity; correction; search, inquiry; collation; discharge (of debt), payment, liquidation
subs: (-kī), To practise purity or nicety; to be nice (about distinctions of caste, &c.); to abstain from touch or contact (with).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Purificatory, purifying, cleansing; purgative; corrective; paying off a debt; retaliating; — a purifier, refiner; a corrector; (in Arith.) the subtrahend; the quantity to be subtracted from a number to render it capable of yielding an exact square root.

s.m. The act of cleaning, cleansing, purifying, refining (of metals); clearance; freeing from faults or errors, correction; expiation; justification; payment, discharge (of a debt), liquidation; search, inquiry, investigation, sifting, scrutiny; — adj. ( = S. शोधनः) Clearing, purifying; purgative; corrective; clean, pure (person)
subs: (-kā), To purify, cleanse to refine (metals); to correct; to justify; to pay off (a debt), to liquidate &c.

def: adj. (f. -ā or -ī), Purifying, &c. ( = śodhak, q.v.); — s.m. Cleaning, cleansing, purifying, refining; clearance; correction, freeing from faults, clearing away errors, removing or eradicating anything prejudicial; precise determination; payment, discharge (of a debt), liquidation, quittance, acquittance; expiation; retaliation, punishment; (in Arith.) subtraction; excrement, fæces, ordure; green vitriol.
s.f. A broom, besom.
adj. Purifying; pure, clean (person).

ety: sodh˚ = S. शोधय(ति), caus. of rt. शुध्
def: v.t. To cleanse, clarify, purify; to refine (metals); to clear of, to correct; to remove, take away; to collate; to search, inquire into, explore; to sift, investigate, scrutinize; to calculate; to pay off, discharge (a debt)
v.t. = sodhnā, q.v.
v.t. 1˚ = sodhnā, q.v.; — 2˚ = soṅdhnā, q.v.
adj. Prolitic.
s.m. Opportune moment; leisure; recovery (from sickness)
s.m. Opportune moment; leisure; recovery (from sickness)

ety: Zend śaoka, fr. śuć; S. शोक, rt. शुच्
def: s.m. Sorrow, grief, lamentation, mourning (see sok)
subs: or sog manānā (-kā), or sog-meṅ rahnā (-ke), To be in grief (for), to grieve or mourn (for); to put on mourning
adj. Sorrowful, afflicted, in mourning, mourning, lamenting
s.f. Sorrow, grief, affliction, bereavement, mourning
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀕 (soga), from Sanskrit शोक (śoka). Also influenced by Classical Persian سوگ (sōg). Doublet of شوک (śok).

def: s.m. = śok, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = sokā, q.v.
s.f. corr. of سوغات saug̠āt, q.v.
adj. Sorrowful, grievous, sad; afflicted; — s.m. One who is distressed or afflicted, a mourner, &c.
adj. See s.v. sog.

ety: sogī, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. Mourning clothes, mourning.

ety: Prk. सोहा; S. शोभा, rt. शुभ्
def: s.f. Beauty, elegance, grace; ornament, decoration, dress.

ety: Prk. सोहओ; S. शोभकः, rt. शुभ्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Bright, brilliant, splendid; handsome, beautiful.
s.m. = suhāg, q.v.
s.m. Borax ( = suhāgā, q.v.); — a harrow (see sohnā).
s.f. = suhāgan, q.v.
s.f. = suhāgan, q.v.
s.f. Clearing, weeding.

ety: Prk. सोहणो; S. शोभनः, rt. शुभ्
def: adj. & s.m. Beautiful, handsome, graceful, pleasing, charming; — a lover; friend; — s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (see ḥalwa).

s.m. A file; rasp.
adj. & s.m. = sohan, q.v.
= suhānā, q.v.

adj. = suhāwan, q.v.
= A صحب ṣaḥb, s.m. pl. (of ṣāḥib, q.v.), Companions, associates, &c.

ety: for A. صحبة, inf. n. of صحب 'to associate with,' &c.
def: s.f. Companionship, society, company; an assembly, meeting, association; a fair; discourse, conversation, intercourse; carnal intercourse, coition, cohabitation
subs: s.m. 'One who carries on or promotes intercourse or companionship,' an acquaintance
s.f. Keeping company (with), associating; acquaintance; — sexual intercourse, coition
part. adj. Trained in, or used to, good society; well-bred
or ṣǒḥbateṅ baratnā, or ṣǒḥbateṅ dekhnā (-kī), To be brought up in good society; to be used to society.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صُحْبَت (suhbat), from Arabic صُحْبَة (ṣuḥba).

def: s.f. corr. of صحبت ṣǒḥbat, q.v.
s.m. A companion, comrade.
s.m. A bad or loose character, a profligate, rake, debauchee, vagabond, blackguard, scoundrel.

ety: ˚pan, ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं, प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं
def: s.m. Dissoluteness, debauchery, raking, rakishness; scoundrelism.

ety: ˚pan, ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं, प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं
def: s.m. Dissoluteness, debauchery, raking, rakishness; scoundrelism.
pron. See s.v. so.
pron. See s.v. so.

ety: soh, q.v.+Prk. aff. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः

ety: soh˚ = Prk. सोह(इ) = S. शोभ(ते), rt. शुभ्
def: v.n. To shine; to be splendid, be glorious; to appear or look beautiful; to impart grace (to, -ko); to set off; to become, befit, beseem; to suit, to be fit.

ety: soh˚ = Prk. सोह(इ) or सोहे(इ) = S. शोधय(ति), caus. of rt. शुध्
def: v.t. To clear, weed.
s.f. (dialec.) An omen; augury.
pron. emphat. = so-hī, q.v.s.v. so.
adj. Straight, direct; near, close (as a translation, &c.).
s.f. Straightness, directness.

ety: sojh, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः, cf. sūdhā, and sīdhā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Straight, direct; right, true, accurate; close (as a translation).
s.f. Straightness, directness, &c. ( = sojh).
s.m. Hole (or holes) in a bedstead (through which the strings for bracing it pass).
s.m. Grief, sorrow, affliction, mourning, lamentation, repining, regretting
subs: To grieve, mourn, lament, repine, regret.

ety: prob. fr. S. शुच्; or = sūkhā, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Scorched, blighted; frost-bitten (as crops, &c.)
s.m. Sorrow, grief, sadness, anguish, regret, woe, mourning, lamentation, affliction, distress
subs: (˚ka+ār˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Afflicted with grief or sorrow, sorrow-stricken, pained, grieved, sorrowful; — a very sorrowful person, &c.
(˚ka+āk˚), adj. (f. -ā), Overcome with sorrow, overwhelmed with grief, greatly distressed
s.m. Dissipation or removal of sorrow; — (f. -ā or -ī), a dissipator or remover of sorrow
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Whose principal occupation is grief; — a person filled with grief
adj. (f. -ī), Abounding in sorrow or woe
or śok-vant, or śok-vān, adj. (f. -vatī), Full of grief, sorrowful, &c. ( = śokī, q.v.)
(˚ka+uk˚), The language of grief, mourning, lamentation

def: s.m. corr. of شوش śosh, q.v.
s.m. corr. of śoshaṇ, q.v.
v.t. To soak up, dry up, drink or suck up, absorb; to drink largely, to guzzle.
s.f. Burning; — a revoke at cards; — 2˚ (part.), contrac. of soḵẖta, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of soḵẖtan; and = Zend śukhta, rt. śuć; S. शुचित, rt. शुच्
def: part. Burnt, scorched (Pers. plur. soḵẖtagān); — s.m. A slow match; tinder; a brand, torch; firewood, fuel
subs: soḵẖta-dil, adj. & s.m. 'Heart-consumed,' heart-sore, consumed with the fire of love, or grief, &c.; pained, grieved; love-sick; — grieved one; love-sick one.

def: s.f. Burning, combustion; a burn; vexation, heart-burning.

ety: Pehl. sūkhtan, rt. Zend śuć = S. शुच्; — tan, prob. = S. aff. तं
def: inf. n. To burn, burning.
adj. Fit or de serving to be burnt.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Full of grief, sorrowful, mourning, sad, grieved, distressed, afflicted.
adj. = śokī, q.v.
v.t. = sokhnā, q.v.
s.m. (colloq.) = شولا śolā, q.v.

s.m. The aquatic plant Ӕschynomene aspera or Hedysarum lagenarium (a large perennial water-plant, abundant in tanks and marshes between Calcutta and Hardwār, with thick stems mainly composed of light white pith, which is made into floats, toys, artificial flowers, models of temples, &c., bottle-cases, stopples or corks, and hats, — anglice, 'Solar ṭopīs,' — invaluable as a protection against the sun).

ety: Prk. सोडहं, or सोलहं?; S. षोडशं
def: adj. Sixteen
subs: s.m. The game of hazard (in playing which several lines are drawn on the ground; — syn. soʼā-pāṭī)
(form. plur.), The whole sixteen; by sixteens, in groups of sixteen; sixteen times.

def: s.f. A game played with sixteen cowries.

ety: solah, q.v.+Prk. अमओ, or अवँओ = S. तम+कः
def: adj. (f. -īṅ), Sixteenth.
v.t. To put to sleep, &c. ( = sulānā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Causing or inducing sleep; — a sleeping-draught, an opiate.

s.m. The moon-plant, Sarccstema viminalis, or Asclepias acida; the acid juice of this plant (prescribed to be drunk at sacrifices); nectar, the beverage of the gods; ambrosia; water; the moon; air, wind; camphor; rice-gruel; heaven, sky, ether; — name of a particular mountainous range; name of one of the eight guardian deities (lok-pāl) of the world; an epithet of Kuver; of Yam; and of Monday
subs: soma-pa, somap, s.m. One who drinks the Soma juice; a Soma sacrificer; the performer of a sacrifice; a Pitri of a particular class (said to be especially the progenitors of the Brāhmans); the manes of the Brāhmans
s.m. A vessel in which the Soma juice is kept
s.m. The drinking of the soma juice
s.m. A sort of grass, Saccharum cylindricum
s.m. A drinker of the Soma juice
som-tīrath, s.m. Name of a place of pilgrimage in the west of India
or som-rājī, s.m. The medicinal plant Serratula (or Vernonia) anthelmintica, black cumin-seed
s.m. The juice of the moon-plant, soma-juice

'Soma's lord, the divinity set up by Soma or the Moon'; name of a celebrated Linga, i.e. columnar emblem of Śiva or Mahādev, or of the place or temple where it was set up (this temple was established in the town of Somnāth-pattan, and was one of the twelve celebrated Linga temples which, in various parts of India, are held in especial veneration; it was so famed for its splendour and enormous wealth, that it attracted the attention of the celebrated Maḥmūd of Gaznī, who, in A.D. 1024, under pretext of destroying its idols, carried off its treasures along with its renowned gates)
s.m. Name of a town on the western coast of India, in the peninsula of Kattywār and province of Gujerāt (it was celebrated for the temple of Somnāth above described)
s.f. The last day of a dark fortnight falling on a Monday (celebrated as a great festival and bathing-day)
or soma-vallarī, and soma-vallikā, s.f. The moon-plant, Sarcostema viminalis
s.f. The moon-plant; the shrub Cocculus cordifolius; the medicinal plant Vernonia anthelmintica
vulg. som-yagya, s.m. A soma sacrifice, an offering or libation of the juice of the acid A sclepias.

def: s.m. = som-wār, q.v.s.v. som.
adj. = sā, 'like'; — 2˚ Emphat. particle = hī, q.v.

s.m. A sort of red sugar-cane; the plant Bignonia indica; the river Sone (which rises in the district of Nāgpūr, on the table-land of Amarkanṭak, four or five miles east of the source of the Nerbudda, and running first northerly and then easterly for 500 miles, falls into the Ganges above Patna)
subs: and son-nadī, s.f. The river Sone.

def: adj. & s.m. Red, crimson; red in the face (from passion, &c.); — the colour red or crimson; fire; a sort of red sugar-cane; blood; red-lead; the plant Bignonia indica; the river Sone (see سون son)
subs: s.m. or śoṇ-bhadrā, s.f. The river Sone
s.m. A red gem, a ruby.

ety: i.q. soṅh = Prk. सवहं, or सवहो; S. शपथं, or शपथः
def: s.f. An oath, asseveration (syn. saugand; qasam)
subs: (-kī), To take an oath, to swear (by).

ety: Prk. सुंतो, the substitute for the abl. plur. of the S.
def: postpn. (dialec.) = se, q.v.

ety: so˚ = Prk. सोव(इ), or सुव(इ) = S. स्वप(ति), rt. स्वप्
def: v.n. To sleep; to lie down, repose recline; to die; to lie (with, -ke-sāth), to cohabit (with)
subs: v.n. (intens.) To go to sleep, to fall asleep
v.n. To keep or remain asleep; to go to sleep ( = so-jānā); to be asleep.
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

ety: Prk. सुवणअं, or सोणअं; S. सुवर्ण+कं
def: s.m. Gold
subs: s.f. Gold and silver; riches, wealth; bullion
adj. (f. -ī), Like gold; of a golden hue
adj. & s.m. 'Sweet or pure as gold'; pure, spotless, innocent; — a man of high birth or of great ability
s.m. A delicious morsel; dainty food; an expensive banquet
s.f. 'A golden bird'; a rich person; a fat prize
or son-mākhī, s.f. Gold-stone; marcasite; iron pyrites.
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

Fragrant smell, perfume; a composition of fragrant substances used for washing the hair; pomatum, pomander

def: pron. = so-hī, &c. q.v.s.v. so.

(˚ta+up˚), s.m. 'The red stone,' or 'blood-stone,' the ruby (syn. śoṇ-ratn).

def: s.m. (dialec.), A kind of sweetmeat.
s.f. = sūṅṭ, q.v.

s.f. Dry ginger; — (ironic.) a thing of value, a precious thing (in this sense, considered by some to be a contrac. of sunoṭh or sunoṭ, 'gold-like,' which is prob. a corr. of S. suvarṅa-vat); (met.) a miser, niggard (cf. sanṭh)
subs: s.m. A mixture of dried ginger, ground cumin seed, &c. (used as a digestive; — syn. zīre-kā pānī)
s.f. A caudle of dry ginger, &c. (given to puerperal women)
s.m. Prince of misers; Miser, esq.
To endure or suffer patiently; to wait patiently
Dry ginger mixed with sugar, &c. (for the use of puerperal women).

ety: soṅṭh, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इओ = S. aff. इकः (इन्+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Miserly, niggardly, penurious, hard; — a miser, niggard ( = soṅṭh)
subs: s.m. (ironic.) A banker of worth or credit; a wealthy person.

ety: soṅṭh, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इओ = S. aff. इकः (इन्+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Miserly, niggardly, penurious, hard; — a miser, niggard ( = soṅṭh)
subs: s.m. (ironic.) A banker of worth or credit; a wealthy person.

def: s.m. Ginger-bread, &c. ( = suṅ-ṭhoṛā, q.v.).
s.m. Ginger-bread, &c. ( = suṅ-ṭhoṛā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Miserly, close-fisted, grasping, hard; — a miser, niggard ( = soṅṭh)
subs: s.m. Prince of misers; Miser, Esq.; — adj. Miserly, &c. ( = soṅṭhū).

def: s.m. Stairs, steps; a staircase; a ladder, a scale; — a way, road.
pron. emphat. (dialec.) He (she, or it) too; he (she, or it) also or even; that too ( = so-bhī, q.v.s.v. so).
s.f. = sāwar, and soʼar, qq.v.
s.f. (dialec.) Root; ramification
s.m. corr. of shor, q.v.
s.m. = shora, q.v.
adj. corr. of solah, q.v.; — s.m. corr. of shorā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = solah, q.v.

ety: Prk. सुरट्ठा; S. सौराष्ट्रा, fr. सुराष्ट्रा
def: s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
s.f. A small hole (in a vessel, &c.), an eyelet, a vent.
v.n. To ramify.
s.f. (dialec.), A broom; — an obsequial rite performed on the third day after a death, when the ashes of the deceased are thrown into the river.
s.m. A kind of Hindī metre, the quatrain. (It is an inverted Dohā, i.e. the second and fourth ćurans of the Dohā are made to stand as first and third, and vice versâ.)
s.f. A society.
s.f. The lily; the iris; the pancratium; (met. and in poetry) the tongue.
adj. Iris-coloured, purple, lilac; — s.f. Purple colour, a bluish colour.
s.m. = شوش śosh, q.v.
s.m. Drying up, exsiccation; dryness; emaciation; pulmonary consumption; intumescence.
adj. = śoshak, q.v.
adj. (f. -ikā), Drying up; absorbing, absorbent; avaricious; rapacious.
s.m. = śoshaṇ, q.v.
s.m. A drying up, draining; sucking up, suction; absorption; exhaustion
subs: dry ginger; — one of the arrows of Kāmdev

ety: sos˚, prob. = S. सम्मृष्य(ति), rt. सम्+मृष्
def: v.t. To bear, bear with patience, take patiently, to endure, suffer, feel.
s.m.f. = H سوتا सोता sotā [Prk. सोत्तअं; S. स्रोत+कं],s.m. A stream, rill, rivulet; current, channel; a spring, source (of a river); a fountain, a jet-dʼeau; backwater (of a river); arm (of the sea).
s.m. = H سوت सोत sot [Prk. सोत्तं; S. स्रोतं], s.m.f. A stream, rill, rivulet; current, channel; a spring, source (of a river); a fountain, a jet-dʼeau; backwater (of a river); arm (of the sea).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

ety: imperf. part. of sonā, and = S. सुप्रः; Prk. सुत्तो
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Sleeping, asleep; — s.m. A sleeping person, one who is asleep.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

ety: S. सुप्त+कं; Prk. prob. सुत्तकं or सुत्तअं
def: s.m. Sleep, sleeping.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

def: s.m. = سونٿا soṅṭā, q.v.
s.f. Swelling, intumescence.
s.m. Swelling, intumescence, tumefaction (from disease); dropsy
subs: adj. Destroying or removing swellings, discutient

ety: sot, q.v.+ī = S. aff. इका; cf. Ved. S. स्रोत्या
def: s.f. A small spring; a rill, rivulet; a sluggish stream or pool formed by the overflowing of a river.
s.m. Name of a class of Brāhmans; an individual of that class, a Brāhman versed in the study of the Vedas, or one who follows a particular branch or school of the Vedas.
pron. emphat. (Braj) = so-hī, q.v.s.v. so.
pron. emphat. (Braj) = so-ū, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = soʼar, q.v.

ety: so(nā) = sow(nā)+at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. augment + aff. तिः)
def: s.f. The state of sleeping.
s.m. Fennel, Anethum sowa; dill (of the com. garden species), Anethum graveolens
s.m. = tīja, q.v.
s.f. A brood-mare.

ety: rt. of soḵẖtan; fr. Zend śuć = S. शुच्
def: part. adj. Burning; inflaming, exciting (used as last member of comp., e.g. jigar-soz; dil-soz); — s.m. Burning; heat, inflammation; ardour, passion; affection; heart-burning, vexation; — a stanza of a mars̤iya or elegiac poem, a dirge
subs: s.m. Reciter of an elegy; one who chants a dirge
s.f. The reciting of an elegy; chanting a dirge
s.m. Burning and melting; — an impassioned style in poetry

ety: imperf. part. of soḵẖtan; see soz
def: part. Burning violently; giving excruciating pain; — s.m. A boil; felon.
s.m. Gonorrhœa, clap.

ety: imperf. part. of soḵẖtan; see soz
def: part. adj. Burning, flaming; inflaming; ardent, fervent; affecting, touching.

ety: Pehl. śūzen; Zend śūka; cf. S. शूक
def: s.f. A needle; pricker (of a gun).

ety: imperf. part. of soḵẖtan; — soz, q.v.+anda = Zend añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: part. & n. of ag. Burning, kindling; — a burner, &c.
s.f. Burning, conflagration.
s.f. Quilting, quilted linen; a kind of coverlet, a small quilted (or embroidered) carpet; tambour-work ( = soznī-kā kām).
s.f. Burning, &c. ( = soz; — used as last member of comp., e.g. dil-sozī); a conflagration.
s.f. Burning; inflammation; ardour, fervour; smart, pain; solicitude; vexation; chafing, fretting (syn. jalan).
s.m. Motion, trembling, quivering, throbbing, shaking, vibrating; the quickening of a child in the womb.
s.f. Emulation, rivalry, competition; envy, jealousy.
adj. Emulating, rivalling; emulous, envious; proud.
s.m. Touching, feeling; touch, contact; sexual intercourse; morbid heat; (in Gram.) the five vargas or classes of consonants embracing the letters from क to म inclusive
subs: (˚śa+īn˚), s.m. The function or sense of touch.

def: adj. Admitting of being touched, perceptible by the touch, tangible, palpable, sensible.
adj. Handling, touching; to be touched, tangible, &c. ( = sparśanīya, q.v.).
part. adj. Distinct, clear, apparent, evident, obvious, manifest; intelligible, easy, plain
s.f. = S سپشٿتو स्पष्टत्व spashṭatva, s.m. Distinctness, clearness, plainness, obviousness.
s.m. = S سپشٿتا स्पष्टता spashṭatā, s.f. Distinctness, clearness, plainness, obviousness.
s.m. = S سپهٿا स्फटा sphaṭā, s.f. Expanded hood of a snake.
s.f. = S سپهٿ स्फट sphaṭ, s.m. Expanded hood of a snake.
s.f. Alum (syn. phiṭkarī).
adj. Crystalline; — s.m. Crystal.
s.m. Crystal; quartz
subs: adj. Consisting of crystal, made of crystal, crystalline.

def: s.f. Expanded hood of a snake ( = sphuṭā).
s.m. Breaking, bursting, splitting open; a swelling, boil, tumour; (in the Pūrva-mīmānsā) the eternal sound.
s.m. A swelling, boil, tumour ( = phoṛā).
adj. Trembling, heaving, throbbing, palpitating; shaken, agitated; — flashing, glittering, sparkling, brilliant
subs: s.m.f. A sparkling, diadem; a brilliant head-dress.

def: s.f. Throbbing, palpitating, shaking, throb, palpitation, tremor.
part. Burst, opened, blown, expanded; evident, manifest, apparent; known, understood.
s.f. corr. of sphūrtti, q.v.
s.f. Wish, desire, eager desire, longing; envy, covetousness.
adj. Wishful, desirous, eagerly longing (for), covetous.
part. Touched, felt, handled.
adj. = sparśanīya, q.v.
adj. Having faith, believing (in), trusting, giving credit to; having reverence (for), respecting, venerating.
s.m. corr. of شرادهہ śrāddha, q.v.
s.f. Faith, belief, trust, confidence, reliance, credit; calmness or composure of mind; intimacy; respect, reverence; hope, wish, vehement desire, the longing of a pregnant woman; purity
subs: (d˚hā+an˚), adj. Endowed with faith, faithful, trustful, believing

def: s.f. Worship, adoration; belief, trust, faith, confidence; liking, fondness (for), affection, desire (after).

adj. Faithful, believing; — s.m. A kind of funeral rite or ceremony in honour of the departed spirits of deceased relatives observed at various fixed periods and on occasions of rejoicing as well as mourning (it consists of offerings with water and fire to the gods and manes, and of food and gifts to the relatives present and to the Brāhmaṇs assisting; and is believed essential to the ascent of departed spirits to a world appropriated to the manes and to their residence there); the gifts presented on the occasion of the observance of the ceremony described above
subs: s.m. Any god presiding over funeral rites; a Viśva-dev; Yam (lord of the dead); Manu Vaivasvat (brother of Yam)
s.m. An epithet of Manu Vaivasvat ( = śrāddh-dev).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Having faith, faithful, believing, disposed to trust, trustful; desirous, wishful; — s.f. A pregnant woman longing for anything.
part. adj. To be trusted, trustworthy, reliable, worthy of confidence.

ety: prob. fr. S. श्राद्ध; but cf. sarāwak
def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Hindūs.
s.f. = srak or sraj, q.v.; — 2˚ (old H.) corr. of svarg, q.v.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit स्वर्ग (svargá). Compare Punjabi سورگ / ਸੁਰਗ (surg). Doublet of سورگ (svarg).

def: s.f. Chaplet, wreath (of flowers), garland.
s.f. = sraj, q.v.
s.m. Exertion, effort, fatigue, toil, pains
s.m. Exertion, effort, painstaking, labour, toil, industry; fatigue, weariness
subs: (˚ma + ār˚), adj. Overcome with fatigue, wearied, exhausted
v.n. To make exertion, to labour, toil; to study
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرْم (šarm), from Middle Persian 𐭱𐭥𐭬 (šʿm /⁠šarm⁠/).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Making effort or exertion, labouring, toiling; following a low or menial business; base, vile, bad; — a devotee, an ascetic, a religious mendicant, a Buddhist ascetic or friar; a beggar.
s.f. A woman of low caste or business; a female mendicant; — a handsome woman.
s.f. = śramaṇā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Making great effort, diligent, laborious; undergoing or incurring weariness; wearying, tiring; — a laborious person, &c.
s.f. (old H.) corr. of srak or sraj, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of asr̤ij, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سرنگ (srng) (c. 1678), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀭𑀼𑀁𑀕𑀸 (suruṃgā), from Sanskrit सुरङ्गा (suraṅgā) or सुरुङ्गा (suruṅgā), ultimately from Ancient Greek σῦρῐγξ (sûrĭnx). Doublet of سِرِن٘ج (siriñj). Cognates Compare with Odia ସୁରଙ୍ଗ (suraṅga), Bengali সুড়ঙ্গ (śuṛoṅgo), Assamese সুৰুঙা (xuruṅa) and Punjabi سُرَن٘گ (suraṉg) / ਸੁਰੰਗ (suraṅg).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Wearied, fatigued, exhausted, distressed; calmed, calm, tranquil.
part. = H سرانتي स्रान्ति srānti, s.f. = śrānt, and śrānti, qq.v.
s.f. Fatigue, weariness, exhaustion.
s.f. = H سرانت स्रान्त srānt, part. = śrānt, and śrānti, qq.v.
s.m. = śāp, and srāp or sarāp, qq.v.
s.m. (old H.) = śrāp, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Sworn; — cursed, accursed.
s.m. A pupil, disciple; a particular class of the Buddhist saints or ascetics; a Bauddh or Buddhist votary (in general); a pupil of a Jain.
s.m. Name of the fourth Hindū month (July-August; vulg. sāwan, q.v.).
s.m. The act of hearing; studying, learning; the ear
subs: (˚ṇa+am˚), s.m. 'Nectar of the ear,' a sweet voice, pleasing sound
(˚na+in˚), s.m. The organ of hearing, the ear.

def: s.m. = śrāvan and sāwan, qq.v.
s.m. The act of hearing; the organ of hearing, the ear; — the twenty-third mansion of the moon (formed of the three principal stars in Aquila)
subs: (magu = Prk. maggu = S. mārgam), s.m. Ear-hole.

def: s.m. = S شرونا श्रवणा śravaṇā, s.f. The twenty-third Nakshatra or lunar mansion (represented by three footsteps, and containing the three principal stars in Aquila).
s.f. = H شرون श्रवण śravaṇ, s.m. The twenty-third Nakshatra or lunar mansion (represented by three footsteps, and containing the three principal stars in Aquila).
s.f. The day of full moon in the month Śrāvaṇ; one of the Hindū domestic sacrifices.
adj. Relating to, or produced in, the month Śrāvaṇ; — s.m. The month Śrāvaṇ.
s.f. The twenty-fourth Nakshatra or lunar asterism (called also Dhanishṭhā, and corresponding to the Dolphin).
v.n. (Braj), To flow, stream, trickle, drop, dribble, leak, ooze.
v.t. (Braj) = sunnā, q.v.
s.f. A chain; a series.
s.f. = شريني śreṇī, q.v.

s.f. A line, row, range, a row of trees, an avenue; a troop, multitude, number, quantity; a corporation, a company of artisans following the same business; a guild or company of traders; a baling vessel, bucket.
adj. Best, most excellent, pre-eminent.
adj. Best, most excellent, excellent, very eminent or illustrious, pre-eminent, chief, higher, or highest, in rank; oldest, senior; — s.m. A Brāhmaṇ; a king; an epithet of Kuver; — cow's milk
s.f. = S شريشٿهتو श्रेष्ठत्व śreshṭhatva, s.m. Betterness, superiority, eminence, excellence.
s.m. = S شريشٿهتا श्रेष्ठता śreshṭhatā, s.f. Betterness, superiority, eminence, excellence.
s.m. = شرييه śreyah, q.v.
s.m. Good fortune, auspiciousness, prosperity, bliss, final happiness, beatitude.
s.m. Good fortune, auspiciousness, prosperity, bliss, final happiness, beatitude.

s.f. Prosperity, happiness, success; improvement; beauty; a name of Lakshmī as goddess of prosperity, &c.; an honorific or respectful prefix to the names of deities and eminent persons, and an auspicious prefix at the beginning of manuscripts, letters, documents, &c. (see śrī)
subs: To commence in the name of Lakshmī, to make a beginning (syn. bismillāh karnā); — to affix (one's) signature (to), to witness (a bond, &c.)
Salutation (or reverence, or glory) to the divine Ganeś (syn. bismillāhi-raḥmani-raḥīm), an invocation to Ganeś
s.m. Name of a tribe of Kāyasths
adj. (f. -ī), Whose prosperity is destroyed; whose beauty or splendour has disappeared. (For other compounds, see s.v. śrī.)

def: s.f. Prosperity, happiness, success; wealth, property; beauty; light, lustre, splendour, glory; decoration; intellect; — name of Lakshmī, wife of Vishṇu, and goddess of plenty and prosperity; name of Sarasvatī; the three objects of life collectively, viz. love, duty, and wealth, &c. (see srī)
subs: s.m. The wood-apple tree, Aegle marmelos, and its fruit
A kind of magical circle; the circle of the earth
s.m. The third of the Rāgas or personified musical modes (it is appropriated to the afternoon in winter, and proceeds from the womb of the earth)
s.m. 'Holy Ranga,' a name of Vishṇu

śrī-mān, śrī-mant, adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Possessed of fortune, fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy, opulent; famous, illustrious; beautiful, pleasing; — an epithet of Vishṇu (as husband of Śrī); of Kuver (the god of wealth); of Śiva; a title applied to any exalted or venerable person; — a famous man, &c.
s.f. A famous or a beautiful woman; — name of the mother of Mādhavāćāryan-name of Rādhā
s.m. The intoxication produced by prosperity
s.m. Epithet of the seventh year of Jupiter's cycle of sixty years; — the word śrī written on the back of a letter
s.m. 'City of fortune,' name of several Indian towns
s.m. 'The dweller with Śrī,' epithet of Vishṇu

s.m. 'The favourite of the goddess of fortune,' epithet of Vishṇu; — a particular mark (or curl of hair) on the breast of Vishṇu or Krishṇ (and used as his mark or symbol); — a hole made through a wall by a house-breaker; — name of one of the lunar asterisms
s.f. The sunflower, Heliotropium indicum (so called as held in the hand of Lakshmī)

adj. (f. -ā), Attended or endowed with prosperity, prosperous, happy, fortunate, auspicious; wealthy, opulent; famous, illustrious; — śrī-yut, An honorific title; your Honour. (For other compounds see srī.)

def: s.m. A jackal ( = siyār, q.v.).
s.m. A jackal ( = siyār); a rogue; a coward; a demon; an epithet of Krishṇ.
s.m. Maker, creator, former, producer.
s.m. Creating, making, forming, producing
subs: and sarjan-hār, and sirjan-hār, or sirjan-hārā, s.m. Creator, maker, former, producer; the Creator
s.m. God the Creator, the Almighty.

def: v.t. To cause to produce or make ( = sirājnā).

ety: sr̤ij˚ or sirij˚, &c. (trans.) = S. सृज(ति), rt. सृज्; — (as an intrans. or pass.) = S. सृज्य(ते), pass. of rt. सृज्
def: v.t. To create, produce, form, make, invent; — v.n. To be created, &c.
s.m. = شرگال śr̤igāl, q.v.
adj. See s.v. شري śrī.
adj. See s.v. شري śrī.
s.m. = śr̤iṅg and sīṅg, qq.v.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سرنگ (srng) (c. 1678), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀭𑀼𑀁𑀕𑀸 (suruṃgā), from Sanskrit सुरङ्गा (suraṅgā) or सुरुङ्गा (suruṅgā), ultimately from Ancient Greek σῦρῐγξ (sûrĭnx). Doublet of سِرِن٘ج (siriñj). Cognates Compare with Odia ସୁରଙ୍ଗ (suraṅga), Bengali সুড়ঙ্গ (śuṛoṅgo), Assamese সুৰুঙা (xuruṅa) and Punjabi سُرَن٘گ (suraṉg) / ਸੁਰੰਗ (suraṅg).

def: s.m. = śr̤ing, q.v.

s.m. A horn (vulg. sīng); top or summit of a mountain, peak, crag; precipice, height, cliff; dignity, supremacy, sovereignty; a fountain or artificial spout of water; any horn-like vessel or instrument; a lotus; a mark, token, sign
subs: s.m. Ginger; — name of a town.

def: s.m. = śr̤iṅgār, and singār, qq.v.

s.m. Love, sexual passion, erotic sentiment; sexual union, coition; — a mark or marks with red-lead on an elephant's head and trunk by way of ornament; a mark; fragrant powder for the dress or person; red-lead; cloves (see singār).

adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the passion of love, erotic; feeling love or amorous passion, full of love, amorous, enamoured, impassioned, lascivious; gaily dressed; stained with red-lead; — an amorous person, an impassioned lover, &c.; — dress, decoration, ornament.
s.m. A place where four roads meet; — the aquatic plant Trapa bispinosa (vulg. singhāṛā).
s.m. A place where four roads meet; — the aquatic plant Trapa bispinosa (vulg. singhāṛā).
adj. = śr̤iṅgī, q.v.
adj. Horned; crested; peaked; — s.m. A mountain; an elephant; a tree; — an epithet of Śiva; name of a saint (r̤ishi), son of Gautama.
s.m. Gold for ornainents; — s.f. A species of sheat-fish, Silurus singio
subs: or śr̤ingī-kanak, s.m. Gold for ornaments.

def: s.f. A cow.
s.m. corr. of, 1˚ śr̤iṅkhal; 2˚ śr̤iṅgār; — 3˚ śr̤igāl.
